what_it_does: This file defines the GenerationWorkspace React component plus helper functions for a structured image-generation job builder. It lets a user pick a target sequence state, generation type, required Vi behaviours, anti-patterns, approved and style-anchor references, rejected negative references, aspect ratio, and image-capable models; it auto-assembles a textual prompt, estimates cost and latency, and offers a Run action to move to review.
observations: React hooks are aliased as useStateGen, useMemoGen, and useEffectGen.
Global state is read from window.MS_DATA and window.ViContext is updated from a useEffect.
Many defaults are hardcoded domain IDs such as targetStateId 'st_05', behaviour IDs, anti-pattern IDs, anchor IDs, and reference IDs.
Image-capable models are discovered from data.providers and toggled individually in the UI.
The autoMulti prop drives a useEffect that selects either all image models or the first one.
Candidates per model are received as a prop and clamped to 1–6 by setCandidates.
Cost is summed across selected models times candidates; latency is the maximum single-model latency.
The assembled prompt is shown in an editable textarea, and a manual edit shadows the auto-assembled version until Re-assemble clears it.
Rejected mockups are surfaced as negative references when showRejected is true.
The assemblePrompt helper contains hardcoded scenario text about Cost Impact from Podium Option B.
previousMockup is looked up with string interpolation st_0${targetState.step - 1}.
A hardcoded ViNote recommends re-running Candidate 3's prompt.
standouts: The prompt is auto-built from structured, domain-specific selections rather than being purely freeform.
Rejected mockups are explicitly rendered as negative references in the generation context.
Model routing is exposed as a per-model toggle with live cost and latency estimates.
Candidate count is externally owned but locally clamped, creating a split-control pattern.
conventions: Functional React component using hooks and module-level helper functions.
Global data access via window.MS_DATA instead of props or context.
Inline styles mixed with CSS utility classes.
A reusable toggle factory for Set-based state updates.
Default prop values encode concrete domain identifiers.
concerns: Hardcoded IDs and scenario text make the workspace less reusable across sequences.
previousMockup lookup assumes single-digit step numbers via st_0${step - 1}.
setCandidates silently clamps input and ignores parse failures.
The models useEffect depends on allImageModelIds, which is computed once and would not reflect provider changes after mount.
Manual prompt state shadows assembledPrompt, so stale edits can persist across selection changes until Re-assemble is clicked.
Cost/latency estimate uses max latency and simple per-image cost, not total wall-clock or batch pricing.
There is no guard for a missing targetState before rendering.
notes: A structured prompt-builder workspace that assembles image-generation jobs from curated states, behaviours, anti-patterns, and references while routing multiple models and previewing cost.
questions: Where else are assemblePrompt, findModel, and providerForModel used or tested?
How is the candidatesProp/onSetCandidates pair wired to the Tweaks panel mentioned in the comment?
Are the hardcoded default IDs valid for every sequence, or only the current one?
How does window.ViContext integrate with the rest of the application?
What should happen when targetStateId does not match any state?
purpose_vs_actual: The header comment describes a generic structured prompt builder with live preview; the file fulfills that shape, but the assembled prompt content and default selections are hardcoded to a specific scenario, so it is less generic than the stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Structured generation jobs over freeform prompts
intention_signals: Provide a studio workspace where a user composes an image-generation job from curated domain primitives, routes it across multiple image models, and reviews an editable auto-prompt plus cost estimate before running it.
novelty: The prompt is assembled from structured selections including required Vi behaviours, rejection-pattern anti-patterns, and explicit rejected-mockup negatives, with live multi-model routing and cost preview.
contribution: A concrete UI pattern for composing structured image-generation jobs: selecting target states, toggling required behaviours and anti-patterns, choosing reference mockups including rejected negatives, routing multiple image models, and previewing the assembled prompt plus estimated cost and latency before running.
summary_for_embedding: GenerationWorkspace is a React workspace that builds structured image-generation jobs from sequence states, Vi behaviours, anti-patterns, mockup references, and model routing, auto-assembling an editable prompt with cost and latency preview.
intention_for_embedding: Enable users to compose, route, and preview structured image-generation jobs before sending them to review.

symbols.does: Clamps candidate count to 1–6 and forwards it via onSetCandidates.
symbols.description: setCandidates is a local wrapper around the onSetCandidates prop. It validates the input, clamps it between 1 and 6, and only calls the parent callback if it exists. It represents the local boundary of the candidates-per-model control.

symbols.does: Factory returning a function that adds or removes an id from a Set state.
symbols.description: toggle abstracts the common pattern of mutating a React Set state: it captures the current Set and its setter, returns a handler that creates a new Set, flips membership of the given id, and invokes the setter. It is reused for behaviours, anti-patterns, references, anchors, and models.

symbols.does: Looks up a model object across all providers by id.
symbols.description: findModel iterates over window.MS_DATA.providers and their models arrays to return the first model matching the supplied id, or null if none is found. It supports cost and latency calculations as well as model routing display.

symbols.does: Returns the provider id that owns a given model id.
symbols.description: providerForModel scans each provider's models array and returns the provider id of the first provider containing the model, defaulting to 'openai' if no match is found. It is used to render provider badges in the model routing list.

symbols.does: Builds a textual generation prompt from structured job selections.
symbols.description: assemblePrompt reads the active target state, generation type, selected Vi behaviours, anti-patterns, reference and anchor ids, and a previous mockup, then concatenates them into a formatted prompt string. It also embeds hardcoded scenario context, material language, content requirements, and prohibitions specific to the Counterpart sequence.
